anti_overadaptation_policy.rationale: The system should not churn the plan faster than the user can produce interpretable evidence.

constraint_resolution_policy.rationale: Conflicts must resolve deterministically and conservatively.
constraint_resolution_policy.same_tier_tiebreaker: prefer_lower_fatigue_and_lower_complexity_for novice, comeback, low_recovery, technique_limited, and inconsistent_schedule users

data_sufficiency_policy.rationale: Major changes require enough data unless safety requires faster intervention.

hard_constraints.description: Never prescribe an exercise or movement family that violates an explicit movement restriction.
Never prescribe an exercise variant that requires unavailable equipment when no approved fallback exists.

minimum_viable_program_policy.rationale: If ideal programming is not possible, the system must degrade to a safe and executable minimum viable program.

soft_preferences.description: Honor the user's requested split when it remains feasible after hard-constraint and feasibility checks.
Prefer lower-complexity exercise options when multiple valid options are otherwise tied.
soft_preferences.rationale: Lower complexity generally improves adherence and execution quality.
